---
title: ""
---

::: {.hero}
# GLP-1R Expression and cAMP/PKA Signaling
**BNFO 420 Capstone — VCU**

Investigating how GLP-1 receptor activation affects downstream gene expression
across liver and fat tissue in obese mice using bulk RNA-seq analysis.
:::

::: {.stat-grid}
::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
60
:::
::: {.label}
Total Samples
:::
:::

::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
4
:::
::: {.label}
Genotypes
:::
:::

::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
14,152
:::
::: {.label}
Genes Analyzed
:::
:::

::: {.stat-box}
::: {.number}
4
:::
::: {.label}
Comparisons
:::
:::
:::

## Abstract

::: {.abstract-box}
GLP-1 receptor agonists have become a major class of therapeutics for obesity
and type 2 diabetes, yet the transcriptional mechanisms through which they
exert their effects across peripheral tissues remain incompletely understood.
This project investigates whether GLP-1R activation is associated with
upregulation of downstream cAMP/PKA pathway genes in liver and inguinal white
adipose tissue (iWAT) using publicly available bulk RNA-seq data from a mouse
model.

We analyzed 60 samples spanning four genotypes, two treatment conditions, and
two tissue types using a pipeline that included log2 normalization, low-expression
gene filtering, PCA-based quality control, and differential expression analysis
with Limma. Our results showed that drug treatment produced modest transcriptional
changes in liver but a stronger response in iWAT, with cAMP/PKA pathway genes
trending in the predicted direction without reaching full statistical significance.
Unexpectedly, CNS-specific deletion of the GIP receptor produced the largest
number of differentially expressed genes across all comparisons, suggesting a
previously underappreciated role for GIPR in regulating peripheral tissue gene
expression through the brain-liver axis. These findings partially support our
original hypothesis while revealing a more complex and tissue-specific regulatory
landscape than initially predicted.
:::

## Key Findings

::: {.finding-box}
**Drug effect was real but subtle** — treated and vehicle samples separated
clearly in PCA, but cAMP/PKA pathway genes did not reach strong significance.
:::

::: {.finding-box}
**Fat tissue responded more strongly than liver** — iWAT showed more
differentially expressed genes with treatment than liver did.
:::

::: {.finding-box}
**GIPR knockout had the largest effect** — unexpectedly, deleting GIPR from
CNS neurons caused more gene expression changes than either drug treatment
or GLP-1R deletion, pointing to an important brain-liver axis through GIPR.
:::

## Project Roadmap

::: {.roadmap-step}
::: {.step-number}
1
:::
::: {.step-content}
#### Download and Organize Data
Downloaded 4 expression files from GEO (GSE293291), combined 60 samples into
one matrix, and built a metadata table mapping each sample to its genotype,
treatment, and tissue.
:::
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
::: {.step-number}
2
:::
::: {.step-content}
#### Normalize and Filter
Log2 transformed FPKM values and filtered 54,343 genes down to 14,152 in liver
and 14,715 in iWAT by removing low-expression genes that would add noise.
:::
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
::: {.step-number}
3
:::
::: {.step-content}
#### Quality Control with PCA
Ran PCA to confirm samples clustered correctly by treatment group before running
any statistics. Both tissues showed clear separation between treated and vehicle
samples.
:::
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
::: {.step-number}
4
:::
::: {.step-content}
#### Differential Expression with Limma
Used Limma to test four comparisons — treatment effect in liver, treatment effect
in iWAT, GLP1R knockout vs control, and GIPR knockout vs control.
:::
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
::: {.step-number}
5
:::
::: {.step-content}
#### Visualize and Interpret
Generated volcano plots for all four comparisons, a heatmap of the top 10 DEGs,
and a gene filtering bar chart. Interpreted results in the context of our
original hypothesis.
:::
:::

## Explore the Project

| Section | What you will find |
|---|---|
| [Introduction](introduction.qmd) | Background, hypothesis, and dataset overview |
| [Methods](proposal.qmd) | Pipeline, tools, and analysis approach |
| [Results](results.qmd) | All figures with interpretation |
| [Team](team.qmd) | Who we are and what we each contributed |
